payload.title: Phase Journal: schema + SQLite store (phase_notes / phase_note_refs)
payload.summary: Add idempotent planning-SQLite migration for phase journal tables, indexes, and a typed store API used only inside task-engine (no raw table reads for agents).
payload.description: Implement tables `phase_notes` and `phase_note_refs` per PHASE_JOURNAL.md (phase_key join key, provenance, idempotency_key partial unique index when feasible). Wire DDL through existing kit SQLite migration path (prepareKitSqliteDatabase / migrateKitSqliteSchema patterns). Implement internal helpers: create note idempotently, list/filter, dismiss, supersede; preserve existing planning/task-engine behavior when tables empty.
technicalScope: SQL migration: phase_notes + phase_note_refs + required indexes from PHASE_JOURNAL.md
Store module under src/modules/task-engine/phase-journal/ (or agreed layout): types, persist, idempotent insert
Unit/integration tests: migration idempotency, CRUD, idempotency key replay
No new agent-facing commands in this slice — persistence only
acceptanceCriteria: phase_notes and phase_note_refs exist with columns and indexes as specified in PHASE_JOURNAL.md MVP
Store supports create (idempotent), list by phase_key/status, dismiss, supersede without corrupting unrelated rows
Migration reruns cleanly; relational + planning open paths unchanged for workspaces without notes
Agents still have no stable read contract to raw tables — internal module tests only until commands land in T100029
payload.risk: Medium — shared planning SQLite; migration ordering must not break existing user_version chain.

payload.title: Phase Journal: MVP commands (add/list/get-phase-context/dismiss/supersede)
payload.summary: Expose Phase Journal through workspace-kit run commands with validation, bounded projections, manifest + instructions, and task-engine dispatch wiring.
payload.description: Implement add-phase-note, list-phase-notes, get-phase-context (deterministic relevance scoring per journal), dismiss-phase-note, supersede-phase-note. Enforce token limits (summary 280, details 1200, refs max 10, default list limits). Infer phaseKey from taskId when possible. Wire resolvePhaseJournalCommands in planning dispatch. Update builtin-run-command-manifest.json, instructions/*.md, run-contract schemas/tests as required by repo standards.
technicalScope: Command handlers + argv validation + JSON envelopes consistent with task-engine
Secret-safety / redaction hooks if project utility exists; otherwise instruction-level prohibitions
get-phase-context: scoring table from PHASE_JOURNAL.md (deterministic tie-break)
Tests: manifest parity, happy paths, validation failures, idempotent add-phase-note
acceptanceCriteria: All five MVP commands registered and callable via wk run with documented JSON shapes
Validation enforces note types, statuses, priorities, lengths, ref counts; phase/task existence when checkable
list/get return bounded projections — not raw SQLite rows
Instruction files + manifest entries + contract checks updated; CI gates pass
Default policy: non-sensitive reads/writes per PHASE_JOURNAL policy table unless configured otherwise
payload.risk: Medium — surface area + contract drift if manifest/schemas not updated.

payload.title: Phase Journal: agent-session-snapshot phaseJournal summary
payload.summary: Add additive, bounded phaseJournal block to agent-session-snapshot when phase is known or inferable — summary only, no full details.
payload.description: Extend agent-session-snapshot read model per PHASE_JOURNAL.md: phaseKey/phaseLabel, counts (active, critical, open follow-ups), topNotes (max 3 default) without details body. Exclude non-active by default. Deterministic ordering.
technicalScope: Snapshot assembler changes + schema/version bump if contract versioned
Tests: golden JSON fragments for snapshot with/without notes
Docs: agent read contract note that agents must use get-phase-context for depth
acceptanceCriteria: snapshot.phaseJournal present only when phase context resolved; omits heavy fields
At most default limits for notes; deterministic output
Existing snapshot consumers unchanged when journal empty
Contract/tests document additive shape
payload.risk: Low–medium — additive JSON; avoid breaking consumers assuming fixed keys.

payload.title: Phase Journal: get-next-actions phaseContext (non-authoritative hints)
payload.summary: Attach bounded phaseContext / taskSuggestionsFromNotes to get-next-actions without overriding task selection rules.
payload.description: Per PHASE_JOURNAL.md: include relevantNotes and taskSuggestionsFromNotes arrays with strict caps. Notes explain context only — ready queue ordering, status, and deps remain authoritative.
technicalScope: Next-actions builder integration + relevance reuse or shared helper with get-phase-context
Tests: selection unchanged when notes present; suggestions bounded
Documentation in instruction or .ai runbook cross-link
acceptanceCriteria: get-next-actions includes phaseContext fields when phase known; bounded and deterministic
No note can force ready task ordering or mask blocked state
Empty journal yields no meaningful regression vs prior payloads
Tests cover note suggestion caps
payload.risk: Medium — risk of accidentally coupling suggestion UX to automation; keep advisory.

payload.title: Phase Journal: optional phaseNotes on run-transition (transactional)
payload.summary: Extend run-transition to accept optional phaseNotes[] inheriting task phaseKey; same transaction as transition; idempotent keys; omit behavior unchanged.
payload.description: Implement PHASE_JOURNAL.md run-transition attachment: validate phaseNotes same as add-phase-note subset, infer phaseKey from task, fail closed before any write on validation error. Transaction must commit transition + notes together or neither.
technicalScope: Transition command schema + TransitionService / planning transaction boundary
Tests: transition succeeds with notes; fails atomically on bad note; idempotency replay
Instruction + manifest updates for run-transition argv
acceptanceCriteria: phaseNotes omitted → identical behavior to today
Successful transition persists 0..n notes with shared provenance
Validation failure prevents transition side effects
Idempotent replay does not duplicate notes
Tier A policy unchanged for transition approval paths
payload.risk: High — transactional coupling with core lifecycle; requires careful SQLite transaction testing.

payload.title: Phase Journal: propose-tasks-from-phase-notes + convert-phase-note-to-task
payload.summary: Second-pass commands: read-only proposal harvester + convert note → proposed task via existing create-task paths; mark note converted with converted_task_id.
payload.description: Per PHASE_JOURNAL.md: propose-tasks-from-phase-notes scans active follow-up/task-suggestion/risk/blocker notes (bounded). convert-phase-note-to-task creates task with matching phaseKey default, marks note status converted, stores converted_task_id. Cannot bypass lifecycle policy for ready/in_progress without transitions. Optional phase_note_task_suggestions table may be deferred — if deferred, document follow-up task id in metadata.
technicalScope: Command implementations + reuse create-task internals / allocateId
Policy alignment with create-task for proposed vs sensitive states
Tests: conversion marks note; duplicate convert rejected; proposal command read-only
Consider deferring task_suggestions table to metadata-only MVP with explicit journal follow-up
acceptanceCriteria: propose-tasks-from-phase-notes never persists tasks by default
convert to proposed task succeeds and updates note row atomically
Direct promotion beyond proposed follows existing transition/policy rules only
Converted notes excluded from default active queries
Manifest/instructions/contracts updated
payload.risk: Medium — task creation policy + evidence paths must stay consistent.
